mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic services in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing configurations.

steps.title: Add the Elastic Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Elastic Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install ECK.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace elastic-system

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the ECK operator.
> Installs the ECK operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the ECK operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the ECK operator is running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> Lists all pods in the elastic-system namespace to verify the ECK operator is running.

steps.title: Deploy an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

```

> Creates an Elasticsearch cluster named 'my-elasticsearch'.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Elasticsearch cluster is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Lists all Elasticsearch clusters in the elastic-system namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Elasticsearch clusters are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade the ECK operator, including backing up existing configurations.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > elasticsearch-backup.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> Backs up the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the ECK operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > elasticsearch-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests/limits in the Elasticsearch CRD configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release may have failed due to a version mismatch.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm chart version and ensure compatibility with your Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n elastic-system' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.13, ensure that your Elasticsearch configurations are compatible with version 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
